steps.title: Translate the pricing into an expression
steps.content:
The first person costs 11 dollars. The remaining $n - 1$ people each cost 7 dollars, contributing $7(n-1)$ dollars.

Total charge: $g(n) = 11 + 7(n - 1)$.

steps.title: Sanity-check with $n = 4$
steps.content:
Direct cost: 11 dollars plus $3 \cdot 7 = 21$ dollars, for a total of 32 dollars.
Formula: $g(4) = 7(4) + 4 = 32$. Match.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice at $n = 4$
steps.content:
• A. $g(n) = 18n \Rightarrow g(4) = 72$. Wrong.
  • B. $g(n) = 18n - 11 \Rightarrow g(4) = 61$. Wrong.
  • C. $g(n) = 7n + 4 \Rightarrow g(4) = 32$. Correct, and it matches the structure $11 + 7(n-1)$.
  • D. $g(n) = 7n + 11 \Rightarrow g(4) = 39$. Wrong (treats all $n$ people as additional, then adds 11 dollars).
That confirms C is correct.
